notes: Wrote iOS accuracy validator mirroring ts-accuracy-validator.py structure. Run stats: 488 files scanned, 421 swift blocks + 1 objc block, 18 files with issues, 63 total issues. Top missing refs and analysis: (1) AmityEventRepository.shared (19x), AmityRoomRepository.shared (17x), AmityPostRepository.shared (6x), AmityClient.shared (4x), AmityFileRepository.shared (3x) — ALL EXTRACTOR_BLIND_SPOT: these types ARE in the iOS surface but their static singleton 'shared' property was not captured by the ios-extractor v1 regex. Static stored properties (static var/let shared) are a known extractor gap — the extractor regex only captures func/init/subscript/case members, not static stored vars. The SDK uses the .shared singleton pattern pervasively. Fix: update ios-extractor.py to capture static var/let declarations. (2) import AmityVideoSDK (3x) — POSSIBLE_DRIFT: not in known-valid-imports list; may be a real SDK module or may be an alternate name for EkoStreamPublisher/UpstraVideoPlayerKit. (3) import AmityUIKit4 (2x) — OUT_OF_SCOPE: AmityUIKit4 is a separate UI component library, not the core SDK; docs are correctly importing it. (4) AmityMentionMapper.metadata (2x) — NEEDS_INVESTIGATION: AmityMentionMapper is in surface but metadata is not in its member list. Could be a property added after surface was extracted. (5) AmityCommunityMembership.QueryFilter (1x), SearchFilter (1x) — EXTRACTOR_BLIND_SPOT: likely nested enum types inside AmityCommunityMembership that the v1 extractor did not capture (nested_types is empty in v1). Overall: ~87% of issues (55/63) are the .shared extractor blind spot — once that is fixed, the remaining real drift will be more visible.
